commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh I will be his father, and he shall be my son. If he commits iniquity, I will chasten him with the rod of men, and with the stripes of the children of men: Men, who are not to be entirely destroyed, like the Chanaanites. (Calmet) This is not unlike the human temptation of which St. Paul speaks, 1 Corinthians x. See Psalm lxxii. , and lxxxviii. (Haydock) The rod of men denotes war, and stripes signify those punishments which God inflicts. Jerome, Trad. ) Some parts of this declaration regard Christ; others Solomon, Hebrews i.
